package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "math"
    "net/http"
)

// Negative scenario for unsafe_pointer_cast: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `unsafe.Pointer` cast between incompatible types in non-test, non-generated code.
type CaseUnsafePointerCastDTO struct {
    ID       string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status   string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount   int    `json:"amount"`
    Payload  []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseUnsafePointerCastModel struct {
    ID        string       `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID  string       `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status    string       `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseUnsafePointerCastRepository struct {
    db    *gorm.DB
    sql   *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseUnsafePointerCastModel
}

type CaseUnsafePointerCastService struct {
    repo   *CaseUnsafePointerCastRepository
    client *http.Client
}

type CaseUnsafePointerCastAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeUnsafePointerCast(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseUnsafePointerCastDTO) error {
    // Safe type conversion using standard library — no unsafe.Pointer needed.
    var x float64 = 3.14
    intVal := int(math.Round(x))
    _ = intVal
    // Safe string to byte slice conversion.
    raw := []byte(input)
    c.Data(http.StatusOK, "text/plain", raw)
    return nil
}
